summary: Paul Rosolie discusses his new book, "Jungle Keeper," and the escalating dangers in the Amazon rainforest, including a recent intense encounter with an uncontacted tribe (Mashco Piro, or Namoles) and threats from narco-traffickers. He highlights the critical mission of Jungle Keepers to protect over 300,000 acres of the Amazon, emphasizing the interconnectedness of biodiversity, indigenous cultures, and global climate.

main_entities.name: Jungle Keeper
main_entities.context: Paul Rosolie's new book, detailing intense stories from his time in the jungle, including an encounter with an uncontacted tribe.

main_entities.name: Jungle Keepers
main_entities.context: Paul Rosolie's organization dedicated to protecting the Amazon rainforest, currently safeguarding over 130,000 acres.

main_entities.name: Amazon rainforest
main_entities.context: The primary focus of conservation efforts, described as one of the most special and beautiful places on Earth, facing threats from logging, mining, and drug trafficking.

main_entities.name: Mashco Piro / Namoles
main_entities.context: An uncontacted tribe in the Peruvian Amazon, known for their isolation and use of 7-foot bows and arrows, with whom Paul had a dramatic encounter.

main_entities.name: Narco-traffickers
main_entities.context: Also referred to as the 'Cocaine Mafia,' they pose an extreme danger in the Amazon, growing coca, building airstrips, and actively targeting conservationists like Paul and JJ.

main_entities.name: Las Piedras River
main_entities.context: A high tributary of the Amazon rainforest, the specific location where Jungle Keepers operates and where the uncontacted tribe was encountered.

main_entities.name: Uncontacted tribes
main_entities.context: Indigenous groups living in nomadic isolation in the Amazon, whose continued existence is threatened by external contact and deforestation.

main_entities.name: Anaconda
main_entities.context: The largest, heaviest snake on Earth, which Paul has extensively studied and interacted with, including a 19-foot specimen.

main_entities.name: Ironwood trees (Shihuahuaco)
main_entities.context: Ancient, giant trees in the Amazon, some over 1,000 years old, which are critical to the ecosystem and hold spiritual significance for the uncontacted tribes.

main_entities.name: Mist River
main_entities.context: A phenomenon described as an invisible river of moisture flowing above the Amazon canopy, witnessed by Paul from the top of a giant tree.

main_entities.name: Jane Goodall
main_entities.context: A legendary conservationist who inspired Paul and endorsed his first book, playing a pivotal role in his career and the founding of Jungle Keepers.

main_entities.name: Age of Union
main_entities.context: A philanthropic organization founded by Dax, which provides significant financial and logistical support to Jungle Keepers and other environmental initiatives.

key_details.fact: Paul Rosolie's new book is titled "Jungle Keeper" and is available for pre-order.
Jungle Keepers has successfully protected over 130,000 acres of Amazon rainforest and aims to protect an additional 200,000 acres.
The Mashco Piro tribe killed two loggers in August 2024, as detailed in Paul's book.

Paul had a dramatic, extended encounter with an uncontacted clan of the Mashco Piro (Namoles) in October 2024, which included peaceful communication and a subsequent violent attack on a community member.
The uncontacted tribe uses 7-foot bamboo-tipped arrows and communicates through gestures and a few shared words, referring to themselves as 'Namoles' (brothers).
Paul and his colleague JJ have active bounties on their heads from narco-traffickers due to their conservation work and cooperation with police.
Paul climbed a 160-foot ironwood tree in the pre-dawn darkness to witness the 'Mist River' flowing above the Amazon canopy at sunrise.
Jane Goodall's endorsement of Paul's first book, "Mother of God," was crucial in securing a publisher and launching his conservation career.
Paul rescued a drowning spider monkey from a rushing river by communicating with it in 'broken spider monkey' language, allowing it to accept help.
